questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
How many grams of borax $\left(\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~B}_4 \mathrm{O}_7 .10 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)$ are required to prepare 100 mL of 0.5 M OH- solution? \{Atomic Mass; $B=10.8 \mathrm{amu}, O=16 \mathrm{amu}, \mathrm{Na}=23 \mathrm{amu}\}$
$\mathrm{B}_4 \mathrm{O}_7^{-2} \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{BO}_3+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}$
questions.solution:
Mole of $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$present in 100 mL 0.5 M solution = 0.05 mole

1 mole of borax gives 2 moles of $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$
0.025 mole of borax gives $\mathbf{0 . 0 5}$ mole of $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$

Weight of borax needed is $0.025 \times 381.27=9.53 \mathrm{~g}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
